issue.author: shao-hua-li

comments.author: weiguozhi
comments.body: Thanks for the report! Will investigate it.

comments.author: weiguozhi
comments.body:
It looks unrelated bug triggered by the patch https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/commit/84bcfa0e1b34938d1d11a44e9e17c6e222dd2f42.

The function iterateOnFunction is called 5 times on main. The patch was triggered only once in the first iteration. If I limit the number of call to iterateOnFunction once or twice, the generated code can work correctly, but if I allow 3 times of call to iterateOnFunction, I will get wrong code.

comments.author: weiguozhi
comments.body:
The problem is in function GVNPass::processBlock, it calls EliminateDuplicatePHINodes to delete duplicated PHI instructions. But GVN uses MemoryDependenceResults *MD to maintain memory dependence information, any deleted instructions must notify MD through MD->removeInstruction, like GVNPass::removeInstruction.

But function EliminateDuplicatePHINodes just silently delete PHI instructions without notify MD, and left some garbage in MD. Later when a new PHI instruction is created at the same address as a deleted PHI instruction, MD may automatically (wrongly) contain dependence information for it. When it is used for optimization, wrong code is generated as the reported test case shows.

comments.author: nikic
comments.body: Confirmed that https://gist.github.com/nikic/ebaa9312e4f3b1a451ee74ef2c710407 would fix the issue.

comments.author: nikic
comments.body: I was wondering whether we could make detecting this easier through the use of AssertingVH, but the relevant value is part of `using ValueIsLoadPair = PointerIntPair<const Value *, 1, bool>`, and there's no easy way to use AssertingVH in there. (Just using `std::pair` under `LLVM_ENABLE_ABI_BREAKING_CHECKS` is not enough, because this is also used inside SmallPtrSet, etc.)

issue.title: Wrong code at -O2 on x86_64 (recent regression in GVN pass)

tests.test_body:
define i32 @main(i64 %x, ptr %d, ptr noalias %p) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop.latch, %entry
  br label %loop2

loop2:                                            ; preds = %loop2.latch, %loop
  br label %loop3

loop3:                                            ; preds = %loop3.latch, %loop2
  %t1 = load ptr, ptr %p, align 8
  %t2 = load ptr, ptr %t1, align 8, !tbaa !0
  %t3 = load ptr, ptr %t2, align 8
  %t4 = load i8, ptr %t3, align 1
  %tobool.not2.i = icmp eq i64 %x, 0
  br i1 false, label %loop3.latch, label %for.body.lr.ph.i

for.body.lr.ph.i:                                 ; preds = %loop3
  store i32 0, ptr %p, align 4
  br label %w.exit.loopexit

w.exit.loopexit:                                  ; preds = %for.body.lr.ph.i
  br label %loop3.latch

loop3.latch:                                      ; preds = %w.exit.loopexit, %loop3
  %t5 = load ptr, ptr %p, align 8
  %t6 = load ptr, ptr %t5, align 8, !tbaa !0
  %t7 = load ptr, ptr %t6, align 8
  br i1 false, label %loop2.latch, label %loop3

loop2.latch:                                      ; preds = %loop3.latch
  br i1 false, label %loop2, label %loop.latch

loop.latch:                                       ; preds = %loop2.latch
  store i32 0, ptr %d, align 4, !tbaa !4
  br label %loop
}

!0 = !{!1, !1, i64 0}
!1 = !{!"any pointer", !2, i64 0}
!2 = !{!"omnipotent char", !3, i64 0}
!3 = !{!"Simple C/C++ TBAA"}
!4 = !{!5, !5, i64 0}
!5 = !{!"int", !2, i64 0}
